methodsAttempted.query: site:jyu.fi University of Jyväskylä generative AI staff teaching guidelines

artifacts.summary: Official discovery found public JYU student guidance on AI use in studies, reporting AI use, and AI in information seeking. Staff and researcher AI guidance linked from a public JYU page redirected to login and was recorded as inaccessible. A JSBE faculty-specific page was not used for university-wide claims.

Reporting the Use of Generative AI Tools in Learning Tasks

artifacts.claimText: The University of Jyväskylä has a public general student guideline page for AI-based applications in studies approved by its Education Council on 25 May 2023.

citation.citationTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines

artifacts.claimText: JYU's student guideline says using AI-based applications to assist in studies is in principle permissible, but the teacher responsible for the study unit gives the final instructions.

citation.citationTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines

artifacts.claimText: JYU's student guideline states that artificial intelligence is not a scientific source and is not suitable as a scientific source, for example in theses.

citation.citationTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines

artifacts.claimText: JYU instructs students that if generative AI applications are used in assignments or theses, the work must mention which application was used and how it was used.

citation.citationTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines

artifacts.claimText: JYU's reporting page tells students to follow course-specific reporting guidelines when present and otherwise report the AI tool, version/date, purpose, verification, and their own contribution.

citation.citationTitle: Reporting the Use of Generative AI Tools in Learning Tasks

artifacts.claimText: JYU's AI in information seeking page says students and researchers affiliated with the university need to use AI tools according to JYU guidelines and policies, while staff and researcher policy links were login-gated during this crawl.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: On 25 May 2023, the Education Council of the University of Jyväskylä approved these guidelines as the University's general guidelines for the use of artificial intelligence (AI) applications.

citation.citationTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Using AI-based applications to assist in studies is in principle permissible. The teacher in charge of the study unit's implementation gives final instructions on the use of the AI applications.

citation.citationTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Artificial intelligence is not a scientific source. Artificial intelligence can support studying as according to point 1, but it is not suitable to be used as a scientific source, for example, in theses.

citation.citationTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If generative artificial intelligence applications have been used in completing assignments or theses related to studies, the work must mention which application was used and how it was used.

citation.citationTitle: Using AI-based applications in studies - JYU's instructions and guidelines

artifacts.sourceTitle: Reporting the Use of Generative AI Tools in Learning Tasks
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If your teacher has not prohibited the use of AI in the assignment, please report the use of AI according to the following instructions. If the course has its own reporting guidelines, please follow them.

citation.citationTitle: Reporting the Use of Generative AI Tools in Learning Tasks

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students and researchers affiliated with the university need to utilise AI tools according to the guidelines and policies of the University of Jyväskylä.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim because the public official page states Education Council approval of general AI application guidelines for studies.
Approved because the claim preserves both permissibility and teacher-final-instruction limits.
Approved as a direct academic-integrity claim from the central student guideline.
Approved because the claim closely follows the source's requirement to mention which generative AI application was used and how.
Approved as a reporting-process claim, not as a separate permission claim.
Approved as a source-status/limitations claim because staff and researcher links were official but login-gated during the crawl.

artifacts.title: University of Jyväskylä AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: The staged crawl found public official JYU student guidance for AI-based applications in studies, including Education Council approval, conditional permissibility, teacher-level final instructions, AI reporting requirements, and the statement that AI is not a scientific source. Staff and researcher AI policy links on Uno redirected to JYU login and were recorded as inaccessible.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Uno staff policy and researcher PDF sources redirected to login during this crawl and were not used as evidence.
JSBE faculty-specific AI principles were not used for university-wide claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
